behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned
behaviors.justification: Real failure: system analyzes files it shouldn't. A pure mock of GitHub API and filesystem won't catch bugs in path handling, globbing patterns, symlink traversal, or directory scanning logic. Need a real (temporary) filesystem structure to verify the linter is invoked on exactly the right files.
behaviors.test_description: Mock GitHub API to return a specific list of changed files (e.g., 3 of 10). Create temporary directory with all 10 files. Run the action and verify subprocess.run() (or equivalent linter invocation) is called exactly 3 times with the correct file paths, never with unchanged files.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration-level test catches filtering logic bugs AND file system traversal bugs together. Pure unit test with mocked filesystem would miss symlink or path resolution failures.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment
behaviors.justification: The behavior says 'in the posted PR comment' — need to verify not just the grouping logic but also the successful GitHub API call with correct format. A unit test catches grouping logic; integration catches posting bugs (malformed JSON payload, markdown formatting, API structure).
behaviors.test_description: Mock linter to produce findings with mixed severities (2 errors, 3 warnings, 1 info). Mock GitHub API to capture the posted comment. Verify the comment body contains exactly three sections (errors first, then warnings, then info) with correct structure, and verify the GitHub API was called with properly formatted JSON.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration-level test prevents bugs like: grouping logic works but markdown formatting is broken, or sections are in wrong order in the API payload.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout
behaviors.justification: Performance and timeout are system concerns. Mocking the linter with simplified per-file latency won't catch real bottlenecks (file I/O, regex performance, memory bloat). Real failure: action times out because the real linter is slow on real files. Need actual file I/O and real (or realistic) linter performance.
behaviors.test_description: Create 200 temporary test files. Run the real linter (not mocked) on all of them via the action. Measure total wall-clock time from start to GitHub comment posted. Assert execution completes in < 10 minutes. Ideally run in a GitHub Actions environment to test against actual timeout.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System-level test required because the failure is real performance regression. Unit or Integration tests with mocked linter latency would always pass, hiding slow regressions.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted
behaviors.justification: Real failure: subprocess crash isn't handled, analysis stops partway through. Mocking a Python exception might not test real process behavior: non-zero exit codes, signals (SEGV, TIMEOUT), partial output, stderr handling. Subprocess crashes are OS-level behavior that mocks can miss.
behaviors.test_description: Create a malformed input file (e.g., invalid syntax) that causes the real linter to crash or exit with error code. Run the action on this file plus 5 normal files. Verify: (1) linter is attempted on remaining files even after crash, (2) results are posted for successfully-analyzed files, (3) error state is handled (no hanging, no exit with failure status).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System-level test catches real subprocess error handling that Integration mocks might skip. E.g., a mock exception doesn't test stderr cleanup or partial output buffering.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output
behaviors.justification: Behavior specifies 'in the output' (the posted PR comment). Need to verify both deduplication logic AND correct posting format. Unit test alone won't catch bugs like: logic dedupes correctly but posting format accidentally re-serializes duplicates, or API payload is malformed.
behaviors.test_description: Mock linter to produce duplicate findings on the same line (e.g., two rules both flag line 42). Run the action and verify: (1) deduplication logic removes the duplicate from the data structure, (2) posted comment body shows exactly one entry for line 42, (3) GitHub API receives correct deduplicated JSON.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration-level test prevents bugs where dedup logic is correct but formatting/posting introduces duplicates. Pure unit test of dedup logic alone would miss these.
